Documentation for this format

name: EMSACNIncident
title: EMS ACN Incident
description: Automated Collision Notification (ACN) telematics data for NEMSIS eInjury.11–.29: providing company, incident identifiers, date/time, GPS location, vehicle description, impact and delta velocity data, rollover, seat location, seatbelt use, and airbag deployment reported by the vehicle telematics system.

element.definition: Automated Collision Notification (ACN) telematics data for NEMSIS eInjury.11–.29: providing company, incident identifiers, date/time, GPS location, vehicle description, impact and delta velocity data, rollover, seat location, seatbelt use, and airbag deployment reported by the vehicle telematics system.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: The value of the measured amount. The value includes an implicit precision in the presentation of the value.
element.comment: The implicit precision in the value should always be honored. Monetary values have their own rules for handling precision (refer to standard accounting text books).
element.requirements: Precision is handled implicitly in almost all cases of measurement.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

mapping.map: PQ.value, CO.value, MO.value, IVL.high or IVL.low depending on the value

element.definition: How the value should be understood and represented - whether the actual value is greater or less than the stated value due to measurement issues; e.g. if the comparator is "<" , then the real value is < stated value.
element.requirements: Need a framework for handling measures where the value is <5ug/L or >400mg/L due to the limitations of measuring methodology.
element.meaningWhenMissing: If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.isModifierReason: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because the comparator modifies the interpretation of the value significantly. If there is no comparator, then there is no modification of the value

binding.description: How the Quantity should be understood and represented.

element.definition: A human-readable form of the unit.
element.requirements: There are many representations for units of measure and in many contexts, particular representations are fixed and required. I.e. mcg for micrograms.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.short: System that defines coded unit form
element.definition: The identification of the system that provides the coded form of the unit.
element.requirements: Need to know the system that defines the coded form of the unit.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: A computer processable form of the unit in some unit representation system.
element.comment: The preferred system is UCUM, but SNOMED CT can also be used (for customary units) or ISO 4217 for currency.  The context of use may additionally require a code from a particular system.
element.requirements: Need a computable form of the unit that is fixed across all forms. UCUM provides this for quantities, but SNOMED CT provides many units of interest.

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: An Extension
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.23. Usage: Optional. Telemetry-predicted high probability of severe injury. NEMSIS YesNoValues mapped to boolean.

element.comment: The definition may point directly to a computable or human-readable definition of the extensibility codes, or it may be a logical URI as declared in some other specification. The definition SHALL be a URI for the Structure Definition defining the extension.

element.definition: Value of extension - must be one of a constrained set of the data types (see [Extensibility](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/extensibility.html) for a list).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

element.definition: Automated Collision Notification (ACN) telematics data for NEMSIS eInjury.11–.29: providing company, incident identifiers, date/time, GPS location, vehicle description, impact and delta velocity data, rollover, seat location, seatbelt use, and airbag deployment reported by the vehicle telematics system.

element.short: eInjury.11 - ACN System/Company Providing ACN Data
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.11. Usage: Optional. Name of the telematics company providing the ACN data.
NEMSIS eInjury.12. Usage: Optional. Unique incident identifier assigned by the ACN provider.

element.short: eInjury.13 - ACN Call Back Phone Number
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.13. Usage: Optional. Phone number for contacting the ACN provider or vehicle occupants. Repeating (0..M).
NEMSIS eInjury.14. Usage: Optional. Date/time of the collision as reported by the telematics system.
NEMSIS eInjury.15. Usage: Optional. GPS coordinates reported by the telematics system in NEMSIS GPSLocation format: 'latitude,longitude' in signed decimal degrees.

element.short: eInjury.16 - ACN Incident Vehicle Body Type
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.16. Usage: Optional.
NEMSIS eInjury.17. Usage: Optional.
NEMSIS eInjury.18. Usage: Optional.
NEMSIS eInjury.19. Usage: Optional.

element.short: eInjury.20 - ACN Incident Vehicle Model Year
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.20. Usage: Optional. Year-precision date (e.g., 2024).
NEMSIS eInjury.21. Usage: Optional. NEMSIS YesNoValues (9923001 No / 9923003 Yes) mapped to boolean.

NEMSIS eInjury.22. Usage: Optional. Change in velocity at impact reported by vehicle telemetry; NEMSIS VelocityUnit attribute determines MPH (UCUM [mi_i]/h) or KPH (km/h). Repeating (0..M) — one repetition per impact.

element.short: eInjury.23 - ACN High Probability of Injury
element.comment: NEMSIS eInjury.23. Usage: Optional. Telemetry-predicted high probability of severe injury. NEMSIS YesNoValues mapped to boolean.
NEMSIS eInjury.24. Usage: Optional. Principal Direction of Force to nearest clock reading: integer 1–12 (12 = frontal collision, 3 = passenger/right side).
NEMSIS eInjury.25. Usage: Optional. ACNYesNoType (Y/N) mapped to boolean.
NEMSIS eInjury.26. Usage: Optional. Patient seat location reported by vehicle telemetry.
NEMSIS eInjury.27. Usage: Optional. ACNYesNoType (Y/N) mapped to boolean.
NEMSIS eInjury.28. Usage: Optional. ACNYesNoType (Y/N) mapped to boolean.
NEMSIS eInjury.29. Usage: Optional. ACNYesNoType (Y/N) mapped to boolean.
